channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader who has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Noem aware Mexican, Canadian leaders have own 'political environments,' but Trump 'means business' on tariffs
item.description: Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said on Sunday that she knows Mexican and Canadian leaders have their own “political environments” but that President Trump “means business” on tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: A man with a Palestinian flag who climbed London's Big Ben tower is arrested
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A man was arrested early Sunday after he climbed the Big Ben tower at the Palace of Westminster in central London while barefoot and holding a Palestinian flag.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Honduras holds primaries as voter frustration simmers over security and the economy
item.description: Voters in Honduras will select candidates from the three main parties to compete in November’s general election for the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Marlon González, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' during fourth week in hospital, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis has apparently shown "slight improvement" and is responding will to oxygen therapy, the Vatican said on Saturday. This is the pope's fourth week at Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea unveils its first nuclear-powered submarine
item.description: North Korea revealed its first nuclear-powered submarine, posing a threat to the United States and South Korea. Kim Jong Un has inspected its construction progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Impeached South Korean leader freed from prison
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison Saturday after a court ruled his detention over insurrection charges was invalid. The conservative president faces charges for his December effort to impose martial law that thrust the country into chaos.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: 1 dead and several injured as tropical low tracks west across Australian east coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Flooding rains are set to continue lashing the Australian east coast even though it avoided the destructive winds of its first tropical cyclone in 51 years, officials said Saturday. One person was confirmed dead and several were injured.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN draft report on children in conflict zones raises eyebrows with framing of Israelis
item.description: The U.N.'s annual report on children in conflict zones, which is set to be published in June, is already getting attention for its framing of Israeli victims and for omitting Hamas crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clashes in Syria between government forces and Assad loyalists kill more than 200
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Fighters siding with Syria's new government stormed several villages near the country's coast, killing dozens of men in response to recent attacks on government security forces by loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, a war monitor said.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cyclone Alfred weakens as it closes in on Australia's east coast
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred was downgraded to a tropical low weather system Saturday as it closed in on Brisbane, Australia. Over 19,000 people have been evacuated, and one remains missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In 2015, Obama committed the US to achieving UN global goals by 2030. Trump just rejected the goals
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In 2015, then-President Barack Obama committed the United States to achieving newly adopted U.N. global goals by 2030, including ending poverty, achieving gender equality and urgently tackling climate change.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court orders South Korean President Yoon freed from jail for martial law trial
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was ordered to be released from jail by a South Korean court after the legal period for his formal arrest expired before his indictment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU leaders commit to working together after Trump signals that Europe must defend itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday committed to working together to bolster the continent's defenses and to free up hundreds of billions of euros for security after U.S. President Donald Trump’s repeated warnings that he would cut them adrift to face the threat of Russia alone.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese hackers, officials charged in cyberespionage campaign
item.description: The hacking by workers of I-Soon was done in some cases at the direction of China's Ministry of Public Security.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: James Harrison, whose blood saved 2.4M babies dies, at 88
item.description: An Australian man credited with saving 2.4 million babies through his record-breaking blood plasma donations over six decades, has died at 88, his family said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump again attacks Zelenskyy with potential peace deal in limbo
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
